"Diving Duck","Anatidae (subset: primarily diving lineages within Anatidae)","Diving ducks are ducks that commonly forage by diving beneath the surface for aquatic vegetation, invertebrates, or fish. "Dobsonfly","Corydalidae","Dobsonflies are large megalopteran insects in the family Corydalidae. "Inchworm","Geometridae","Inchworms are the caterpillar (larval) stage of geometer moths (Geometridae). They are named for their characteristic “looping” movement: lacking mid-abdominal prolegs, they bring the rear end forward to form a loop, then extend the front end—often described as measuring or “inching” along. Many are excellent twig mimics and may hold themselves stiffly at an angle when disturbed.","https://a-z-animals.com/media/2021/12/geometridae-on-plant-in-the-wild-picture-id900342298-1024x535.jpg","inchworm","[REDACTED]s are the caterpillar (larval) stage of geometer moths (Geometridae). "Pyrosome","Pyrosomida","Pyrosomes are pelagic, free-floating colonial tunicates (urochordates).
